steps.title: Block the two main shapes
steps.description: Lightly sketch a small circle for the head and a larger oval for the body, slightly tilted. The head should be roughly one-third the size of the body.

steps.title: Connect them
steps.description: Draw a short curve to join the head to the body — this is the neck. Notice the gesture: most perched birds have a gentle S-curve through the body.

steps.title: Outline the silhouette
steps.description: Trace the full outline of head and body in one connected shape. Add a small triangular beak and a tail extending from the back of the body.

steps.title: Place the eye and beak details
steps.description: Add a small dot for the eye, positioned roughly where the head meets the beak. Refine the beak so the upper and lower halves meet cleanly.

steps.title: Suggest the wing
steps.description: Add a curved line on the body to mark where the wing tucks in. A few short strokes near the tail can hint at feathers without rendering each one.

steps.title: Add the perch
steps.description: Draw a horizontal branch under the bird and two small lines for the legs gripping it.
